enrichment.short_description: Actual Budget is a free, open-source, local-first envelope budgeting app you can self-host for full data privacy.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Actual Budget: free, open-source envelope budgeting app with bank sync, multi-device sync, and self-hosting for privacy.

features.title: Envelope Budgeting
features.description: Budget based on actual income using envelope-style budgeting, assigning every dollar a job.

features.title: Bank Sync
features.description: Connect bank accounts via GoCardless (EU/UK) or SimpleFIN (US/Canada) for transaction syncing.

features.title: Multi-Device Sync
features.description: Sync budget data across multiple devices with optional end-to-end encryption.

features.title: Transaction Import
features.description: Import transactions from QIF, OFX, QFX, CAMT.053, and CSV files.

features.title: Custom Reports
features.description: Track net worth and cash flow using a custom report engine.

features.title: YNAB Migration
features.description: Import existing budgets from YNAB4 and nYNAB.

features.title: Developer API
features.description: Build custom tools and integrations against Actual's API.

features.title: Self-Hosting
features.description: Run your own sync server via Docker, Fly.io, or a hosting provider like PikaPods.

pros: Completely free and open source under the MIT license
Self-hosted option gives full control over financial data
Local-first architecture continues to work offline
Cross-platform desktop apps for Windows, Mac, and Linux
Active open-source community with crowdsourced translations via Weblate
cons: Multi-device sync requires self-hosting a sync server, which takes technical setup
No confirmed official first-party mobile apps at time of research
Relies on community/Discord support rather than dedicated customer service

faqs.question: Is Actual Budget free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Actual Budget is 100% free and open source under the MIT license.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Actual Budget?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can run your own sync server via Docker, Fly.io, or a hosted option like PikaPods.

faqs.question: Does Actual Budget support bank syncing?
faqs.answer: Yes, via GoCardless for EU/UK accounts and SimpleFIN for US/Canada accounts.

faqs.question: Can I migrate from YNAB?
faqs.answer: Yes, Actual Budget supports importing data from YNAB4 and nYNAB.

faqs.question: What platforms does Actual Budget run on?
faqs.answer: It's available as downloadable apps for Windows, Mac, and Linux, plus deployable via Docker.

use_cases.title: Privacy-conscious envelope budgeting
use_cases.description: Individuals who want zero-based envelope budgeting without sending financial data to a third-party cloud service.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted personal finance
use_cases.description: Technical users who want to run their own budgeting server on their own infrastructure via Docker.

use_cases.title: YNAB alternative
use_cases.description: Former YNAB4/nYNAB users looking for a free, open-source replacement with data migration support.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Actual Budget?
paragraphs: Actual Budget is a free, open-source personal finance application built around envelope budgeting, where users assign every dollar of income to a category.
It uses a local-first architecture, meaning the app keeps working even without a network connection, and data can be synced across devices through a self-hosted sync server.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Actual Budget is for
paragraphs: Actual Budget suits privacy-focused users and self-hosters who want a free, open-source alternative to subscription budgeting apps like YNAB, and are comfortable running or renting a small sync server.

name: Maybe Finance
skip_reason: The maybe-finance/maybe GitHub repository was archived by the owner on Jul 27, 2025 with a notice that it 'is no longer actively maintained.' The company publicly pivoted away from the B2C personal finance product to an unrelated B2B financial-forecasting/scenario-planning tool in 2026, stopped monthly billing, and refunded remaining B2C subscribers. maybe.co now presents this different B2B product ('Know your business, in plain English'), so Maybe Finance no longer exists as a usable personal-finance product under that name today.

enrichment.short_description: Lunch Money is a paid, web-first personal budgeting and net-worth app with bank sync, multi-currency, and crypto tracking.
enrichment.starting_price: $10/month (or $60/year minimum on the pay-what-you-want annual plan)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Lunch Money is a paid personal budgeting app with bank sync, multi-currency and crypto tracking, and a 30-day free trial.

features.title: Bank Sync
features.description: Automatically sync transactions from most US and Canadian banks via Plaid, with CSV upload or manual entry as fallback.

features.title: Multi-Currency Support
features.description: Track accounts and transactions across multiple currencies.

features.title: Budgeting with Rollover
features.description: Set category budgets with a redesigned interface for category setup and rollover tracking.

features.title: Crypto Portfolio Tracking
features.description: Track cryptocurrency holdings alongside traditional bank accounts.

features.title: Rules Engine
features.description: Automatically categorize and tag transactions using custom rules.

features.title: Calendar View
features.description: View income and expenses in a calendar-based interface with transaction detail.

features.title: Developer API
features.description: Programmatic access to account and transaction data.

features.title: Net Worth Tracking
features.description: Calculate net worth across all connected accounts.

pros: All features included at every price point, with no feature-gated upsells
Pay-what-you-want annual pricing option
Supports multi-currency and crypto portfolio tracking
Web-first app with companion iOS and Android apps
30-day free trial before any payment is required
cons: No permanent free plan — a paid subscription is required after the trial
Bank sync coverage is strongest in the US/Canada and still expanding in Europe
Web-first design means the browser/desktop experience is the primary interface

faqs.question: How much does Lunch Money cost?
faqs.answer: $10/month, or an annual pay-what-you-want plan with a $60/year minimum.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Lunch Money offers a 30-day free trial before a subscription is required.

faqs.question: Does Lunch Money support multiple currencies?
faqs.answer: Yes, multi-currency support is included on every plan.

faqs.question: Can I track cryptocurrency in Lunch Money?
faqs.answer: Yes, crypto portfolio tracking is included.

faqs.question: Is Lunch Money available on mobile?
faqs.answer: Yes, it has companion apps for iOS and Android in addition to the web app.

use_cases.title: Multi-currency budgeting
use_cases.description: People who travel or hold accounts in multiple currencies and need unified budget tracking.

use_cases.title: All-in-one net worth tracking
use_cases.description: Users who want to combine bank accounts, crypto, and manual accounts into one net-worth view.

use_cases.title: Rules-based transaction categorization
use_cases.description: Users who want automated categorization and tagging of transactions via a rules engine.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Lunch Money?
paragraphs: Lunch Money is a paid, web-first personal budgeting and net-worth tracking app founded in 2019 and based in Toronto, Canada.
It syncs bank transactions via Plaid, supports multiple currencies and cryptocurrency accounts, and includes a rules engine for automatic categorization, with all features available on every plan.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Lunch Money is for
paragraphs: Lunch Money suits people who want a paid, all-inclusive budgeting tool without tiered upsells, especially those managing multi-currency accounts or wanting to track crypto alongside traditional finances.

enrichment.short_description: Ghostfolio is an open-source wealth management app for tracking investments, offered free self-hosted or as a paid managed cloud service.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Ghostfolio: open-source, self-hostable wealth management software for tracking transactions, portfolio performance, and risk.

features.title: Transaction Management
features.description: Create, update, and delete transactions across multiple investment accounts.

features.title: Portfolio Performance Tracking
features.description: Track Return on Average Investment (ROAI) across timeframes including Today, WTD, MTD, YTD, 1Y, 5Y, and Max.

features.title: Risk Analysis
features.description: Static analysis to identify potential portfolio risks and view portfolio composition.

features.title: Import/Export
features.description: Import and export transaction data.

features.title: Dark Mode & Zen Mode
features.description: Alternate UI modes including a distraction-free Zen Mode.

features.title: Progressive Web App
features.description: Mobile-first PWA design usable across devices without a native app install.

features.title: Self-Hosting via Docker
features.description: Deploy on linux/amd64, linux/arm/v7, or linux/arm64 via Docker Compose, with community images for Umbrel, CasaOS, Runtipi, Unraid, and Home Assistant.

features.title: Ghostfolio Premium
features.description: A managed cloud-hosted version that bundles access to professional market data providers.

pros: Free, AGPLv3-licensed self-hosted option
Managed Ghostfolio Premium cloud option removes the need to self-host
Progressive Web App works across devices without a native app
Wide community-supported deployment options (Umbrel, CasaOS, Runtipi, Unraid, Home Assistant)
Active open-source project with thousands of commits and hundreds of releases
cons: Self-hosting requires running PostgreSQL and Redis alongside Docker infrastructure
Exact Ghostfolio Premium pricing was not consistently published in an easily verifiable form at time of research
Professional market-data provider access appears limited to the paid Premium tier

faqs.question: Is Ghostfolio free?
faqs.answer: The self-hosted version is free and licensed under AGPLv3; a paid Ghostfolio Premium cloud-hosted version is also available.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Ghostfolio?
faqs.answer: Yes, via Docker Compose, with community packages available for Umbrel, CasaOS, Runtipi, Unraid, and Home Assistant.

faqs.question: What does Ghostfolio track?
faqs.answer: Transactions, portfolio performance (ROAI) across multiple timeframes, and portfolio risk/composition.

faqs.question: Is Ghostfolio open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's open-source software licensed under AGPLv3 and developed publicly on GitHub.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted portfolio tracking
use_cases.description: Technical users who want to track their investment portfolio without relying on a third-party cloud service.

use_cases.title: Managed wealth tracking
use_cases.description: Users who want investment performance and risk tracking without managing their own server, via Ghostfolio Premium.

pricing_plans.price: Paid — see ghostfol.io/en/pricing for current rate

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Ghostfolio?
paragraphs: Ghostfolio is an open-source wealth management application, first published in 2021, for tracking investment transactions, portfolio performance, and risk.
It can be self-hosted for free under the AGPLv3 license via Docker, or used through Ghostfolio Premium, a managed cloud offering that bundles professional market data providers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Ghostfolio is for
paragraphs: Ghostfolio suits individual investors who want a transparent, open-source way to track portfolio performance and risk, whether they prefer self-hosting their own instance or paying for the managed Premium cloud service.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, self-hosted Kanban board tool for team task management with a paid Pro tier and managed cloud hosting.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Planka: open-source, self-hosted Kanban board software with real-time boards, a free Community edition, and paid Pro/cloud plans.

features.title: Kanban boards
features.description: Unlimited boards and cards for organizing team tasks in a Trello-style interface.

features.title: Real-time synchronization
features.description: Board updates sync live across all connected users.

features.title: Self-hosted deployment
features.description: Deploy Planka on your own server via Docker; source code is available on GitHub.

features.title: REST API
features.description: A documented REST API (with Swagger UI reference) allows programmatic access, plus community Python and PHP SDKs.

features.title: OpenID Connect authentication
features.description: Supports OIDC-based single sign-on.

features.title: Multilingual interface
features.description: Interface available in 35+ languages.

features.title: Custom branding (Pro)
features.description: Pro tier adds custom logo, colors, and login page branding.

features.title: Calendar and map views (Pro)
features.description: Pro tier unlocks calendar and map views plus recurring cards.

pros: Free, open-source Community edition that can be fully self-hosted
Active project with 12.2k+ GitHub stars and regular releases
Docker-based deployment (8.1M+ image pulls)
Real-time collaborative boards with a modern UI
Optional managed cloud hosting if you don't want to self-host
cons: Free self-hosted Community edition lacks calendar/map views, recurring cards, and custom branding
Community edition license prohibits cross-company use
Pro features (branding, guest roles, advanced views) require a paid license
Hosted plans bill per-user with additional charges for extra storage and guests

faqs.question: Is Planka free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Community edition is free to self-host under Planka's Fair Use license, though it has some feature restrictions compared to Pro.

faqs.question: Does Planka offer a hosted/cloud version?
faqs.answer: Yes, Planka offers managed cloud hosting for both the Community and Pro editions, priced per month plus per-user fees.

faqs.question: Is Planka open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, Planka's source code is publicly available on GitHub under a fair-code licensing model.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Planka with Docker?
faqs.answer: Yes, Planka is commonly deployed via Docker, with over 8.1 million image pulls reported.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted team task boards
use_cases.description: Teams that want full control over their data by running Planka on their own infrastructure.
use_cases.audience: Small to mid-size engineering or ops teams

use_cases.title: Managed Kanban without ops overhead
use_cases.description: Organizations that want Planka's board features without managing servers, using the hosted Community or Pro plans.

use_cases.title: Branded multi-client boards
use_cases.description: Agencies needing custom-branded boards and cross-company usage rights via the Pro tier.

features: No calendar/map views, recurring cards, or custom branding

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Planka?
paragraphs: Planka is a modern, Trello-style Kanban board application for organizing and visualizing team tasks. It is open source, with a free self-hosted Community edition and a commercial Pro tier that adds branding, guest roles, and additional views.
The project is maintained by PLANKA Software GmbH (Kempten, Germany) and can be deployed via Docker on your own infrastructure, or run as a managed cloud instance through PLANKA's hosted plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Planka is for
paragraphs: Planka suits teams that want a self-hosted alternative to Trello-style boards, from small teams using the free Community edition to agencies and enterprises that need custom branding, guest access, or cross-company usage rights via the Pro tier.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source, cross-platform project management and issue tracking web application built on Ruby on Rails.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Redmine is a free, open-source project management and issue-tracking web app with Gantt charts, wikis, time tracking, and SCM integration.

features.title: Issue tracking
features.description: Tracks tasks and bugs with customizable statuses and workflows across multiple projects.

features.title: Gantt charts and calendars
features.description: Built-in Gantt chart and calendar views for project scheduling.

features.title: Role-based access control
features.description: Per-project roles and permissions for users.

features.title: Wikis and forums
features.description: Project-specific wikis and discussion forums for documentation and collaboration.

features.title: Time tracking
features.description: Log and report time spent on issues and projects.

features.title: SCM integration
features.description: Integrates with SVN, CVS, Git, Mercurial, and Bazaar repositories.

features.title: REST API
features.description: Documented REST API supporting XML and JSON for issues, projects, wiki pages, and search.

features.title: Custom fields
features.description: Custom fields for issues, time entries, projects, and users.

pros: Completely free and open source under the GNU GPL v2
Mature, long-running project with roots dating back to 2006
Highly customizable through a large ecosystem of community plugins
Supports multiple SCM systems (Git, SVN, Mercurial, CVS, Bazaar) and multiple database backends
Cross-platform, Ruby on Rails-based web application
cons: Requires self-hosting and ongoing server/technical maintenance since there is no official managed cloud plan
Plugin compatibility varies across Redmine versions and may need testing/updates when upgrading
No official paid support tier from the core redmine.org project itself

faqs.question: Is Redmine free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Redmine is free, open-source software distributed under the GNU General Public License v2.

faqs.question: Does Redmine integrate with Git or SVN?
faqs.answer: Yes, Redmine has built-in SCM integration supporting Git, SVN, CVS, Mercurial, and Bazaar.

faqs.question: Is Redmine self-hosted only?
faqs.answer: The official redmine.org project is self-hosted; you install and run it on your own server.

faqs.question: Can Redmine be extended with plugins?
faqs.answer: Yes, Redmine supports a plugin architecture, with plugins available via an official directory and on GitHub.

use_cases.title: Software issue tracking
use_cases.description: Development teams tracking bugs and feature work across multiple projects.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted project management
use_cases.description: Organizations that want full control of their project data on their own infrastructure.
use_cases.audience: IT teams and organizations with data-control requirements

use_cases.title: Multi-project portfolio tracking
use_cases.description: Teams managing several projects with shared roles, custom fields, and Gantt-based scheduling.

features: Full source code access under GPL v2
Issue tracking, Gantt charts, wikis, time tracking

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Redmine?
paragraphs: Redmine is a flexible, free and open-source project management web application written in Ruby on Rails. It is cross-platform and cross-database, and has been developed since 2006.
It combines issue tracking with Gantt charts, calendars, wikis, forums, time tracking, and integration with source control systems like Git and SVN.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Redmine is for
paragraphs: Redmine fits software teams and organizations that want a mature, no-cost, self-hosted project and issue tracker with deep customization through plugins and custom fields, and are willing to manage their own server infrastructure.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source, self-hosted developer toolkit forked from Phabricator, covering code review, task tracking, and repository hosting.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Phorge is a free, open-source, self-hosted fork of Phabricator offering code review, task tracking, and repository hosting for dev teams.

features.title: Code review
features.description: Differential-style code review tooling inherited from Phabricator.

features.title: Repository hosting
features.description: Built-in hosting for source code repositories.

features.title: Task tracking
features.description: Bug and task tracking for software projects.

features.title: Herald automation
features.description: Rule-based automation engine for triggering actions on project events.

features.title: Wikis
features.description: Documentation/wiki tooling for team knowledge bases.

features.title: Self-hosted LAMP/LEMP deployment
features.description: Runs on your own PHP/MySQL or MariaDB server stack (Linux, macOS, or BSD).

pros: Free and open source under the Apache 2.0 license
Continuation of the well-regarded Phabricator toolset after its original discontinuation
Actively maintained by the community, including PHP 8+ compatibility work and security patches
Proven at scale: the Wikimedia Foundation migrated its production task tracking to Phorge
Combines code review, task tracking, and repo hosting in one self-hosted platform
cons: Requires self-managing a LAMP/LEMP stack (PHP, MySQL/MariaDB) rather than offering a hosted SaaS option
No Windows support; requires a Unix-like OS (Linux, macOS, or BSD)
Smaller community footprint than mainstream project-management tools
No official managed cloud/hosted plan found

faqs.question: Is Phorge free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Phorge is free and open source under the Apache 2.0 license.

faqs.question: Is Phorge actively maintained?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is developed by a community team; the Wikimedia Foundation completed a production migration to Phorge for task tracking, and maintainers continue shipping PHP 8+ compatibility and security fixes.

faqs.question: What is Phorge's relationship to Phabricator?
faqs.answer: Phorge is a community-maintained fork of Phabricator, the developer toolsuite originally built at Facebook, which became unmaintained in mid-2021.

faqs.question: Can Phorge run on Windows?
faqs.answer: No, Phorge requires a Unix-like operating system such as Linux, macOS, or BSD.

use_cases.title: Migrating off deprecated Phabricator
use_cases.description: Teams previously on Phabricator moving to an actively maintained, compatible fork.
use_cases.audience: Engineering teams that ran self-hosted Phabricator

use_cases.title: Integrated code review and task tracking
use_cases.description: Organizations wanting code review, repository hosting, and task tracking in a single self-hosted platform.

use_cases.title: Data-sovereign engineering tooling
use_cases.description: Large organizations needing full control over their engineering collaboration data on their own infrastructure.
use_cases.audience: Large engineering orgs, e.g. foundations and enterprises

features: Self-hosted deployment on your own LAMP/LEMP stack

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Phorge?
paragraphs: Phorge is a free, open-source, community-maintained fork of Phabricator, the developer toolsuite originally built at Facebook that became unmaintained in mid-2021. Phorge's community fork effort began in 2021, with an initial public release in September 2022.
It provides code review, repository hosting, task tracking, wikis, and rule-based automation (Herald) in a single self-hosted platform, released under the Apache 2.0 license.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Phorge is for
paragraphs: Phorge is for engineering teams that relied on Phabricator and need an actively maintained, self-hosted continuation, and for organizations that want integrated code review and task tracking under their own infrastructure. The Wikimedia Foundation is a notable production adopter.

enrichment.short_description: Taskwarrior is a free, open-source command-line task manager for organizing and tracking to-do lists.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Taskwarrior is a free, open-source command-line to-do list manager with filters, priorities, tags, and recurring tasks.

features.title: Command-line interface
features.description: Manage your entire task list from the terminal with a fast, scriptable command syntax.

features.title: Filters and reports
features.description: Search and narrow tasks with filters, and display them using customizable reports.

features.title: Tags and virtual tags
features.description: Organize and categorize tasks using user-defined and built-in virtual tags.

features.title: Contexts
features.description: Group and filter tasks by context to focus on specific areas of work.

features.title: Recurrence
features.description: Create repeating tasks with configurable recurrence rules.

features.title: Urgency scoring
features.description: Tasks are automatically prioritized using an urgency algorithm based on due dates, tags, and other attributes.

features.title: User Defined Attributes (UDAs)
features.description: Extend tasks with custom fields to fit specific workflows.

features.title: Hooks API and syncing
features.description: Extend behavior with external scripts via the hooks API, and sync tasks across devices.

pros: Completely free and open source (MIT license)
Fast, scriptable command-line workflow suited to power users and GTD practitioners
Highly extensible via hooks, UDAs, and a large ecosystem of add-on tools
Packaged for Linux, macOS, and Windows through common package managers
Active, long-running project with a large GitHub community
cons: No graphical interface out of the box; requires comfort with the command line
Steeper learning curve than mainstream to-do apps for non-technical users
Native mobile apps are not part of the core project
Syncing setup (Taskserver) requires additional configuration

faqs.question: Is Taskwarrior free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Taskwarrior is free and open-source software released under the MIT license.

faqs.question: What platforms does Taskwarrior support?
faqs.answer: Taskwarrior is packaged for Linux/Unix distributions (including Arch, Debian, Fedora, Ubuntu), macOS (via Homebrew), and Windows.

faqs.question: Does Taskwarrior have a graphical user interface?
faqs.answer: Taskwarrior's core product is a command-line utility; it does not ship a built-in GUI.

faqs.question: Can I sync tasks across multiple devices?
faqs.answer: Yes, Taskwarrior supports syncing tasks across devices.

faqs.question: How long has Taskwarrior been in development?
faqs.answer: Development on Taskwarrior began in 2006 and continues to be actively maintained.

use_cases.title: GTD-style personal task management
use_cases.description: Track to-do lists, priorities, and contexts using the Getting Things Done methodology.
use_cases.audience: Individuals who prefer command-line tools for personal productivity

use_cases.title: Developer and sysadmin workflows
use_cases.description: Manage tasks alongside other terminal-based workflows without leaving the shell.

use_cases.title: Scripted and automated task tracking
use_cases.description: Use hooks and UDAs to integrate Taskwarrior into custom scripts and automation pipelines.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Taskwarrior?
paragraphs: Taskwarrior is free and open-source software that manages a to-do list from the command line, described by its creators as flexible, fast, and unobtrusive.
It is developed by Gothenburg Bit Factory and has an active ecosystem that includes companion tools such as Timewarrior for time tracking and Taskshell.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Taskwarrior is for
paragraphs: Taskwarrior suits developers, sysadmins, and other command-line-comfortable users who want fast, scriptable task management with deep customization through filters, UDAs, and hooks, rather than a graphical to-do app.

enrichment.short_description: Super Productivity is a free, open-source to-do list and time-tracking app with Pomodoro and task-integration features.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Super Productivity is a free, open-source todo app with time tracking, Pomodoro timer, and Jira/GitHub/GitLab task imports.

features.title: Task management with subtasks
features.description: Organize work with quick-add tasks, subtasks, notes, and due dates.

features.title: Time tracking
features.description: Track time spent on tasks with keyboard shortcuts and detailed work logs.

features.title: Pomodoro timer and focus mode
features.description: Built-in Pomodoro timer and a focus mode to reduce distractions during deep work.

features.title: Multiple board layouts
features.description: Organize tasks with Kanban boards, Eisenhower matrix, compact lists, or custom layouts.

features.title: Issue tracker integrations
features.description: Import and sync tasks from Jira, GitHub, GitLab, Gitea, OpenProject, Linear, ClickUp, and Azure DevOps.

features.title: Repeating tasks
features.description: Set up recurring tasks with smart scheduling.

features.title: Data sync without accounts
features.description: Sync data across devices via Dropbox, WebDAV, or CalDAV without requiring user registration.

features.title: Plugin system
features.description: Extend functionality through plugins.

pros: Completely free and open source (MIT license)
No user accounts, registration, or telemetry required — data stays local by default
Supports Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, iOS, and web
Deep integrations with popular issue trackers like Jira, GitHub, GitLab, and Linear
Combines task management with time tracking and Pomodoro in one app
cons: No built-in cloud accounts; sync relies on user-configured Dropbox or WebDAV
No official hosted/paid tier for teams needing centralized administration
Feature depth (multiple board types, integrations) can add a learning curve for new users

faqs.question: Is Super Productivity free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Super Productivity is free and open source under the MIT license, with no paid tier.

faqs.question: What platforms does Super Productivity support?
faqs.answer: Super Productivity runs on Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, iOS, and as a web app.

faqs.question: Does Super Productivity integrate with issue trackers?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports importing and syncing tasks from Jira, GitHub, GitLab, Gitea, OpenProject, Linear, ClickUp, and Azure DevOps.

faqs.question: Does Super Productivity require an account?
faqs.answer: No, the app requires no user accounts or registration, and data stays on the user's device by default.

faqs.question: How can I sync data across devices?
faqs.answer: Data can be synced across devices using Dropbox, WebDAV, or basic CalDAV integration.

use_cases.title: Developer task and time tracking
use_cases.description: Import issues from Jira, GitHub, or GitLab and track time spent resolving them.

use_cases.title: Deep work and focus sessions
use_cases.description: Use Pomodoro timer and focus mode to structure work sessions and reduce procrastination.

use_cases.title: Personal privacy-first productivity
use_cases.description: Manage tasks and track time entirely offline without creating an account or sending data to a server.
use_cases.audience: Privacy-conscious individuals and local-first software users

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Super Productivity?
paragraphs: Super Productivity is an open-source task management and time-tracking application that combines to-do lists, a Pomodoro timer, and focus mode in one tool.

It runs on Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, and iOS, as well as in the browser, and can import tasks from issue trackers such as Jira, GitHub, GitLab, Gitea, OpenProject, Linear, ClickUp, and Azure DevOps.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Super Productivity is for
paragraphs: It is aimed at developers and individuals who want a privacy-respecting, local-first productivity app that unifies task tracking, time tracking, and focus techniques without requiring an account or subscription.

enrichment.short_description: SiYuan is a privacy-first, self-hosted, open-source personal knowledge management app with an optional paid cloud sync tier.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SiYuan is an open-source, self-hosted note-taking app with block linking, flashcards, and optional paid cloud sync tiers.

features.title: Block-level editing
features.description: Edit notes using 20+ block elements and 10+ inline elements for granular content structuring.

features.title: Bidirectional linking
features.description: Create block-level references and backlinks between notes to build a connected knowledge base.

features.title: Knowledge graph
features.description: Visualize relationships between notes and blocks in a graph view.

features.title: Spaced repetition flashcards
features.description: Built-in flashcard review using the FSRS spaced-repetition algorithm.

features.title: Database/table views
features.description: Create relational database-style views with templates inside notes.

features.title: PDF annotation
features.description: Annotate PDF documents directly within the app.

features.title: Self-hosted or Docker deployment
features.description: Run SiYuan locally or self-host via Docker for full data control.

features.title: Optional cloud sync
features.description: Paid tiers add official cloud storage, data sync/backup, and a cloud inbox; free/PRO tiers support third-party S3 or WebDAV sync.

pros: Core app is fully open source under AGPL-3.0
Self-hostable with full local data control and no forced account
Large, actively developed project on GitHub
Supports desktop (Windows/macOS/Linux), mobile (iOS/Android/HarmonyOS), and Docker/web deployment
Free tier retains core note-taking functions; paid tiers are optional add-ons for cloud sync
cons: Official cloud sync and hosted storage require a one-time paid PRO or subscription tier
Free tier has no official cloud storage, sync, or backup
Interface and community documentation are heavily Chinese-language-centric, though English is supported
Third-party sync (S3/WebDAV) on lower tiers requires users to set up and pay for their own storage service separately

faqs.question: Is SiYuan free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, SiYuan's core app is free and open source (AGPL-3.0), but official cloud sync and storage require a paid PRO or subscription tier.

faqs.question: What does the paid tier include?
faqs.answer: The PRO tier ($64 one-time) adds third-party S3/WebDAV sync and backup. The subscription tier ($148 one-time as listed) adds official cloud sync, 8GB of official cloud storage, cloud asset hosting, and a cloud inbox.

faqs.question: Can I self-host SiYuan?
faqs.answer: Yes, SiYuan can be self-hosted, including via Docker deployment, with data kept under the user's own control.

faqs.question: What platforms does SiYuan support?
faqs.answer: SiYuan supports Windows, macOS, and Linux desktop apps, iOS, Android, and HarmonyOS mobile apps, and Docker/web deployment.

faqs.question: Who develops SiYuan?
faqs.answer: SiYuan is developed by Yunnan Liandi Technology Co., Ltd. under the B3log open-source organization.

use_cases.title: Personal knowledge management
use_cases.description: Build a connected notebook of block-linked notes with backlinks and a knowledge graph.
use_cases.audience: Individuals building a personal wiki or second brain

use_cases.title: Self-hosted note-taking
use_cases.description: Run SiYuan on your own server or desktop via Docker for full control over data without relying on a vendor's cloud.

use_cases.title: Spaced-repetition study
use_cases.description: Convert notes into flashcards and review them using the built-in FSRS spaced-repetition algorithm.

features: No official cloud storage, sync, or backup
Third-party S3 data sync and backup
Third-party WebDAV data sync and backup

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SiYuan?
paragraphs: SiYuan is a privacy-first, self-hosted, fully open-source personal knowledge management application, built with TypeScript and Golang and released under the AGPL-3.0 license.
It offers block-level editing, bidirectional linking, a knowledge graph, spaced-repetition flashcards, and database-style views, and runs on desktop, mobile, and Docker deployments.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SiYuan is for
paragraphs: SiYuan suits users who want a self-hosted, block-based note-taking tool with deep linking and knowledge-graph features, and who are comfortable managing their own sync via third-party storage or an optional paid cloud tier for official sync and backup.
